text:
Once upon a time, in the charming town of Blossom Heights, there lived a peculiarly solitary cat. His name wasn’t much of a story—just Mr. Whiskers.

Mr. Whiskers didn’t like company. He didn’t play much except occasionally bat aside a fly ball with his tiny paw. He didn’t even meow much. His primary mode of communication was a series of purrs that sounded like melancholy violin tunes.

He could spend hours staring out a window, perhaps pondering the mysteries of ancient feline history. Once a year, during the cherry blossom festival, Mr. Whiskers would abandon his loneness, taking occasional walks around Rosebud Park. Visitors marveled at his graceful, shadow-stalking pace.

One morning, after a particularly long contemplational nap, Mr. Whiskers found himself drawn to a peculiar, flickering lantern lamp near the ornamental bamboo shrine. It had a strange pattern of stars twinkling like nebulae
